qas.q: You need to configure a SOHO (Small Office Home Office) network to provide Internet Protocol (IP) Addresses only to devices allowed on the network. You have configured a list of devices that are allowed to access the network. Which feature should be enabled?
In which sector on the hard drive is the Master Boot Record (MBR) located?
qas.a: Answer: In the first sector of the first partition

qas.q: Which of the following Intel CPUs are laptop only chips?
In a large network, to which of the following devices would you NOT assign a static Internet Protocol version 4 (IPv4) address?
In Windows Firewall properties, how can you block a computer from accessing a specific program?
qas.a: Answer: Use the "Allow" state for outbound connections and create a rule to block the program.

qas.q: What is NOT true about troubleshooting WiFi network problems?
qas.a: Answer: There may be a problem with the Ethernet NIC.

qas.q: What causes most paper jams?
qas.a: Answer: Improper media and creases in paper

qas.q: Which of the following cloud services offers an online data center infrastructure without having the associated costs of purchasing and managing the hardware?
Which of these is part of a fiber optic strand?
What is true about RAID hardware configurations?
qas.a: Answer: RAID hardware can be supported by the motherboard.

qas.q: When should you consider a workaround as solution to a non-critical IT problem?
qas.a: Answer: When you want to be most cost-effective

qas.q: Which cloud deployment type is targeted towards only a certain group of users?
Your computer shuts down every time you plug your external USB optical drive to it. After removing the optical drive, it works fine. What kind of problem could this indicate?
Which type of printer allows you to print text files to an image file format (such as a .JPEG) so that you can email an image of the text to a colleague?

You are working on a project that requires virtualizing an operating system (OS) for an end user to test an application. It has been requested that you use a type-2 hypervisor to accomplish this. Which of the below would fulfill that requirement?
What is true about identifying the cause of a problem?
qas.a: Answer: You must balance being methodical with being efficient.

qas.q: A company uses smartcards with Personal Identification Numbers (PINs) as a two-step authentication process to access the network. This is an example of which of the following network authentication methods?
Which of these can cause damage to a hard disk?
Which of the following cases are used for high-end user personal computers (PCs)?
How do you configure a proxy server in Windows?
qas.a: Answer: Click the LAN Settings button on the Connections tab of the Internet Options dialog.

qas.q: Which of the following is the addressing scheme that uses a 32-bit number for each address?
What is the last thing you should always do before considering an incident closed?
qas.a: Answer: Confirm with the customer the problem has been fixed.

qas.q: You plan on purchasing a Windows 10 desktop to virtualize two Linux operating systems for web development purposes. You are in the market for a machine with an Intel processor. What CPU virtualization feature will you look for when shopping?
Which port is typically known as a serial port?
What is the purpose of having an accelerometer in a laptop?
qas.a: Answer: To protect the hard drive

qas.q: You are considering replacing the hard drive in your laptop. You know that the laptop is using a 2.5" drive, but you want to research available drive heights as well. Which of the below is NOT a valid 2.5" hard drive height?
How do you configure a host to use a static Internet Protocol version 4 (IPv4) address in Windows?
qas.a: Answer: Select the "Use the following IP address" option and enter the IP address on the General tab of the IPv4 Properties dialog.

qas.q: You need to do some application testing. You have a Windows 10 desktop and decide to use Hyper-V to create virtual environments to test the applications in. You do not want the guest virtual machines to be able to communicate with any other physical system on the network: however, communication between the guests and the hosts is allowed. Which type of virtual switch should you create in Hyper-V?
Which of the following Hypervisors is included with Linux distributions?
Which feature of a wireless router allows you to prioritize certain types of network traffic?
You want to build a computer that can stand upright on a desk and takes up the least amount of space possible. Which case type should you select?
Which of these has a transfer rate of 1.6 Gigabits per second (Gbps)?
